title: Scandit SDK

sections.markdown: Scandit is a commercial, enterprise-grade barcode + document scanning SDK. Tuned for tough real-world conditions — damaged codes, glare, motion blur, tiny prints — and multi-code batch scanning. Requires a license key.

Scandit outperforms ML Kit on **hostile scanning conditions** — critical for retail POS, warehouse, and GS1 workflows where scan speed × accuracy directly impacts throughput. It's the reason enterprises pay: 2–3× faster scans and near-100% accuracy on damaged codes.
Enabling every symbology + auto-focus tricks 'just in case.' Each extra symbology costs decoder cycles. Enable only what you actually scan (e.g., GS1: EAN-13, GS1-128, DataMatrix).

Manage lifecycle: switch camera OFF between scans, ON only when the scanner is visible. Preload the DataCaptureContext once at app start; instantiating it per screen is slow. Bundle license keys per flavor (dev/prod).

flashcards.front: When choose Scandit over ML Kit?
flashcards.back: Enterprise / GS1 / retail throughput where accuracy on damaged codes and scan speed matter.
Enabling too many symbologies. Enable only what you need.
Switch OFF between screens; ON when scanner is visible.

options: Enterprise accuracy + speed on hostile scans
Bundled per flavor via build config
quiz.explain: Inject per-flavor at build; keep out of git.

challenges.text: Set up a BarcodeCapture with EAN-13 + QR and log reads.
Add MatrixScan-style batch scanning of multiple codes at once.
Optimize scan time for damaged GS1-128 labels.
A/B compare Scandit vs mobile_scanner on a real dataset; report scans/sec + accuracy.
